risk_assessment_summary.methodology: Deterministic rule-based classification with escalation overrides

critical_item_details.regulatory_basis: FSCA Settlement Rule 5.2.1 - Data availability requirements for large exposures

escalation_reasons: CIS data unavailable for large exposure counterparty

critical_items.rationale: CIS data unavailable (CP-007) combined with large exposure (ZAR 55M > ZAR 50M threshold) triggers deterministic Rule R2. Unable to assess counterparty settlement capacity without CIS data.
critical_items.regulatory_requirement: FSCA Settlement Rule 5.2.1 requires real-time counterparty data availability for exposures exceeding ZAR 50M
critical_items.recommended_action: Immediate contact with CP-007 to resolve CIS connectivity and manual verification of settlement capacity

critical_gaps.impact: Unable to assess lending balance and settlement capacity for ZAR 55M exposure

regulatory_compliance_summary.settlement_monitoring_requirement: COMPLIANT - All T+1 and T+2 trades monitored within regulatory timeframes
regulatory_compliance_summary.risk_assessment_requirement: COMPLIANT - Deterministic risk classification applied to all trades
regulatory_compliance_summary.data_quality_requirement: NON_COMPLIANT - CIS data unavailable for 1 counterparty with large exposure
regulatory_compliance_summary.escalation_requirement: COMPLIANT - Human escalation triggered for data quality issue
regulatory_compliance_summary.audit_trail_requirement: COMPLIANT - Complete audit trail generated and stored

next_actions: Resolve CIS connectivity issue with CP-007 (TerraFin Brokers Ltd)
Manual verification of CP-007 settlement capacity for TRD-2007
Monitor trend direction in next pipeline cycle
Review CIS system resilience for large exposure counterparties
